channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pro-Putin leader backs harsh crackdown as over 40 people hospitalized amid protests in Georgia
item.description: Dozens are hospitalized after massive protests broke out across Georgia following the pro-Putin government's move halting decadeslong talks on joining the EU amid increasing ties with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia's protests lay bare a clash of values and interests as it navigates a Russia-West standoff
item.description: Georgia is convulsed by political upheaval again after its pro-Moscow government, fresh off parliamentary elections denounced as rigged by its critics, decided to suspend negotiations for the small former Soviet republic to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stars, heads of state, solemn rituals and high-security celebrations for Notre Dame's reopening
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The reopening of Notre Dame this coming weekend is going to be a high-security affair, with a repeat of some measures used during the Paris Olympics and the sealing-off to tourists of the cathedral's island location in the heart of the French capital.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah fires into Israel-held area, its first strike since ceasefire began
item.description: Hezbollah fired into a disputed border zone held by Israel on Monday, the militant group’s first attack since its ceasefire with Israel took hold last week, after Lebanon accused Israel of violating the truce more than 50 times in recent days. Israel says it has acted to thwart potential attacks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Taiwan president speaks with US officials from Hawaii as China condemns visit
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te touched down in Hawaii over the weekend and spoke by phone with former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) as China slammed the U.S. for the visit and for selling weapons parts to Taipei. Hawaii Gov.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israeli American soldier from Long Island killed: Military
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) -- The Israeli military said Monday an Israeli American soldier who was believed to have been captured alive by Hamas in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack was killed that day and his body taken into the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US Navy ships repel attack from Houthis in Gulf of Aden
item.description: The U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale and USS O’Kane repelled an attack against American ships by Yemen-based Houthi rebels, CENTCOM says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Landmark climate change case opens at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The top United Nations court took up the largest case in its history on Monday, hearing the plight of several small island nations helpless in combating the devastating impact of climate change that they feel endangers their very survival.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American-Israeli IDF platoon commander killed in battle, body held in Gaza, IDF says
item.description: A 21-year-old American-Israeli believed to have been held hostage since October 2023 was confirmed dead by the Israel Defense Forces on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden travels to Africa where policies were ‘over-promised and under-delivered,' amid massive China expansion
item.description: President Biden visits Angola this week and is spending billions to get better supplies of electric vehicle battery metals, but the admin may have already been beaten by China's investments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan's president arrives in Hawaii for a 2-day stop in the US as part of South Pacific visit
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Taiwan's president Lai Ching-te arrived Saturday in Hawaii to begin a two-day transit in the U.S. as part of a trip to the South Pacific, his first since assuming office.
item.dc:creator: TAIJING WU, HUIZHONG WU and AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Over 40 people hospitalized in Georgia during protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — A third night of protests in the Georgian capital against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union left 44 people hospitalized, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria launches counterattacks in an attempt to halt insurgency, as Iran's top diplomat meets Assad
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian military rushed reinforcements to the northwest and launched airstrikes Sunday in an attempt to push back insurgents who seized the country’s largest city of Aleppo, as Iran pledged to help the government counter the surprise offensive.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tiffany Trump's father-in-law Massad Boulos to become senior presidential adviser on Arab affairs
item.description: ​President-elect Trump on Sunday said he would appoint Massad Boulos, a billionaire from Lebanon whose son Michael is married to Tiffany Trump, to serve as his senior adviser on Arab and Middle Eastern affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: Plastic treaty talks draw to a close with production limits still under debate
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiations on a treaty to end plastic pollution are drawing to a close Sunday as nations continue debating whether part of that should be putting limits on plastic production.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military says Hezbollah is smuggling weapons into Lebanon during ceasefire
item.description: Hezbollah terrorists reportedly are smuggling weapons across Lebanon's borders despite an ongoing ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel hits alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli military jets hit alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites near the border crossing between Lebanon and Syria, according to officials, who allege the Lebanese militant group violated a ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump wants a Gaza ceasefire deal 'now,' Graham says
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) said that President-elect Trump wants to reach a ceasefire and hostage deal before inauguration day on Jan. 20, Axios reported on Friday. "Trump is more determined than ever to release the hostages and supports a ceasefire that includes a hostage deal.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Ireland votes in a close-run election where incumbents hope to cling on to power
item.description: Ireland is voting in a parliamentary election that will determine the country's next government; voters are selecting more than 170 lawmakers to sit in the Dail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas attacker opens fire at Israeli bus in West Bank, injuring at least 8: report
item.description: Hamas is claiming responsibility Friday for an attack on a bus near an Israeli settlement in the West Bank, which has left at least eight injured, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: School bus driver charged with holding girl captive for 12 years
item.description: A school bus driver in Colombia allegedly held a girl captive for more than a decade until she recently escaped, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: 2 companies cut ties with Conor McGregor
item.description: Proximo Spirits, the owner of Irish whiskey brand Proper No. 12, will no longer feature McGregor’s name.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel ramps up strikes on Beirut as its leadership prepares to vote on ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: The Israeli military also issued warnings for 20 more buildings in Beirut's suburbs to evacuate before they too were struck
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
